stories.text: As a Rust developer embedding liminal, I want to publish typed messages to a channel through an in-process handle so that I get zero-hop messaging without network overhead.

As a Rust developer embedding liminal, I want to switch from embedded to remote mode by changing configuration rather than code so that I can scale to multi-process deployment without rewriting my application.
As a Rust developer connecting remotely, I want the SDK to automatically reconnect and recover my subscriptions after a network disruption so that my application resumes without manual intervention.
As a Rust developer connecting remotely, I want publish operations to return typed backpressure signals (accept, defer, reject) so that I can implement application-level flow control.

personas.name: Gleam Developer
personas.role: Authoring message types and conversation handlers

stories.text: As a Gleam developer, I want to define message types as Gleam records and have the SDK enforce them at compile time so that type mismatches are caught before deployment.
As a Gleam developer, I want to author conversation handlers as Gleam modules implementing a behaviour so that I can write request-reply and saga logic in a familiar OTP-style pattern.
As a TypeScript developer building a browser app, I want to connect to liminal over WebSocket via WASM so that I can subscribe to channels without a server-side proxy.
As a TypeScript developer, I want generated TypeScript interfaces for channel message types so that I get IDE autocompletion and type checking without writing type definitions manually.
As a TypeScript developer building a server app, I want to use the same SDK API in Node.js as in the browser so that I can share message handling code between client and server.
As a platform operator, I want SDK connection pools to be configurable with max connections, timeouts, and buffer sizes so that I can tune resource usage for my deployment environment.
